vocabulary.zh_pinyin: ni bijiao xihuan rou haishi yu?
vocabulary.translation_en: Do you prefer meat or fish?
no sugar; do not add sugar
What do you want to eat?
What do you want to drink?

vocabulary.zh_pinyin: ni xihuan chi rou haishi yu?
vocabulary.translation_en: Do you like eating meat or fish?
vocabulary.translation_ru: Ты любишь есть мясо или рыбу?

vocabulary.zh_pinyin: wo xihuan zai fanguan chi fan.
vocabulary.translation_en: I like eating in a restaurant.

grammar_patterns.name: Venue words build from food or drink roots plus 馆
grammar_patterns.explanation_en: Lesson 11 explains restaurant and hotel-style vocabulary through a food or drink root followed by 馆, treated here as an establishment suffix.

grammar_patterns.name: Food choice questions pivot around 还是
grammar_patterns.explanation_en: Once the food nouns are introduced, the lesson immediately turns them into either-or questions about what someone prefers to eat.

grammar_patterns.name: 比较喜欢 expresses preference
grammar_patterns.explanation_en: The teacher explicitly glosses 比较喜欢 as liking something more than the alternatives, which is why it works as prefer.

grammar_patterns.name: 最喜欢 marks strongest preference
grammar_patterns.explanation_en: After preference, the class asks how to say what someone likes most and retrieves 最喜欢 as the superlative choice.

grammar_patterns.name: Animal plus 肉 builds meat words
grammar_patterns.explanation_en: Beef, lamb, pork, and chicken are all taught as productive compounds built from the animal root plus 肉.

grammar_patterns.name: 加 handles restaurant add-ons
grammar_patterns.explanation_en: Sugar and milk are handled through 加, while the no-sugar request is framed as either 不要糖 or 不加糖.

grammar_patterns.name: 想 plus 吃 or 喝 asks what someone wants
grammar_patterns.explanation_en: The class recycles the 想 pattern from earlier lessons and turns it into restaurant-ready eat and drink questions.

grammar_patterns.name: Dietary restriction language uses negation plus food nouns
grammar_patterns.explanation_en: The closing block turns food vocabulary into real dietary statements such as not eating meat, eating only vegetables, and liking to eat in a restaurant.

pronunciation_focus.coaching: The lesson explicitly contrasts fish and language by tone. Keep 鱼 as the rising second-tone item and 语 as the lower third-tone language word.
Practice 牛肉, 羊肉, 猪肉, and 鸡肉 as a linked set so the shared 肉 stays stable while only the animal root changes.
Keep the preference chunks smooth and compact. Beginners often pause between 比较 and 喜欢 or between 最 and 喜欢.
Treat 北京烤鸭 as one cultural chunk rather than four isolated syllables. The phrase should sound like a single menu item.
The lesson closes by reminding learners that 不 changes to second tone before another fourth-tone syllable, as in 不吃 or 不加糖.

drills.prompt: Ask in Chinese: Do you prefer meat or fish?
coaching_notes: Use 比较喜欢 for prefer and keep 还是 as the either-or connector.

drills.prompt: Ask in Chinese: What do you want to eat? What do you want to drink?
drills.target_answer: 你想吃什么？你想喝什么？
coaching_notes: Keep the shared 想 pattern and only swap 吃 for 喝.

drills.prompt: Repeat the menu phrase: Peking duck.
coaching_notes: Treat it as one menu chunk, not as four isolated syllables.

drills.prompt: Say in Chinese: I like eating in a restaurant. I do not eat meat.
drills.target_answer: 我喜欢在饭馆吃饭。我不吃肉。
coaching_notes: This is the lesson's most useful dietary pair: where you like to eat and what you do not eat.

drills.prompt: Roleplay a drink order: ask for coffee with sugar, then switch it to coffee without sugar.
coaching_notes: Keep 加糖 and 不加糖 as fast restaurant chunks.

tests.question: Which word in lesson 11 means seafood?
Which lesson-11 phrase means to prefer or like more?
Translate into Chinese: I do not eat meat.
Say the question: What do you want to drink?
What tone reminder does lesson 11 give for 不 before another fourth tone?
tests.answer_key: 不 changes to second tone before another fourth-tone syllable.

teaching_notes: Do not teach lesson 11 as a food glossary. Keep every noun tied to a real choice, request, or restaurant sentence.
Make the sequence preference first, menu item second: 比较喜欢 and 最喜欢 are what turn the food words into real conversation.
Use the meat block as a productive system. Once 牛肉, 羊肉, 猪肉, and 鸡肉 click, the learner sees how compounds work instead of memorizing isolated labels.
Restaurant add-ons should sound automatic. 加糖, 不加糖, and 牛奶 belong in the learner's mouth as service-language chunks.
Close on diet and habit language: 我喜欢在饭馆吃饭 and 我不吃肉 are more useful than listing ten more dishes.

lessons.title: Lesson 12: Ordering, Service Phrases, and Invitation Language

lesson_lines.translation_en: Lesson 12 explicitly resumes the food topic because lesson 11 only opened it and did not cover the restaurant side in full.
The teacher says the class can already distinguish some kinds of meat and say what they prefer.
Before ordering, the lesson returns to the future-intention pattern for saying what you are going to have.
to want; to be going to; to intend to
to come; bring here in ordering language
The class immediately attaches the already-known sugar detail to the order.
A cup of coffee, with sugar.
The lesson now shifts from drinks to taste as a practical restaurant survival block.
spicy / do not make it too spicy
cooked rice; rice as a meal item
I invite you; I am treating you.
lesson_lines.translation_ru: Я приглашаю тебя; я плачу за тебя.

lesson_lines.zh_hanzi: 欢迎来北京 / 欢迎来俄罗斯
lesson_lines.zh_pinyin: huanying lai Beijing / huanying lai Eluosi
lesson_lines.translation_en: Welcome to Beijing / Welcome to Russia
lesson_lines.translation_ru: Добро пожаловать в Пекин / в Россию

lesson_lines.translation_en: Do you prefer bread or rice?
The teacher notes that some northern food words entered Chinese from Russian, with soup given as an example.
I do not drink black tea often.

lesson_lines.zh_hanzi: 喝一点酒 / 很少喝酒
lesson_lines.zh_pinyin: he yidian jiu / henshao he jiu
lesson_lines.translation_en: drink a little alcohol / drink alcohol rarely
lesson_lines.translation_ru: пить немного алкоголя / редко пить алкоголь

lesson_lines.translation_en: What alcohol do you like to drink?
The teacher closes by saying they still need to gather everything they have learned before the final week.

vocabulary.translation_en: to want; to be going to
Do you prefer bread or rice?
What alcohol do you like to drink?

grammar_patterns.name: Ordering toggles between 要 and 来
grammar_patterns.explanation_en: Lesson 12 uses 要 for what you want and 来 for bring me or let it come as a practical ordering frame with a waiter.

grammar_patterns.name: Drinks take 杯
grammar_patterns.explanation_en: A single serving of coffee is built with 杯, which keeps the order compact and natural in restaurant speech.

grammar_patterns.name: Menu and bill are separate service words
grammar_patterns.explanation_en: The lesson insists that 菜单 is the menu while 买单 is the bill or check at the end of the meal.

grammar_patterns.name: Taste adjectives drive food requests
grammar_patterns.explanation_en: The taste block is practical restaurant language: spicy, salty, sweet, bitter, and sour, plus the request not to make something too spicy.

grammar_patterns.name: Toasts use 为……干杯
grammar_patterns.explanation_en: The drinking block packages toasts as 为 plus the person or cause, followed by 干杯.

grammar_patterns.name: Frequency adverbs go before the verb
grammar_patterns.explanation_en: The teacher uses 常常 and 很少 before the verb phrase to say how often something happens, including restaurant and drinking habits.

grammar_patterns.name: Rice versus noodle-family food
grammar_patterns.explanation_en: The lesson distinguishes cooked rice 米饭 from flour-based foods built on 面, such as bread 面包 and noodles 面条.

grammar_patterns.name: 请 covers polite requests, invitations, and paying
grammar_patterns.explanation_en: The same 请 family stretches from please sit and please come in to I invite you and I am treating you.

pronunciation_focus.coaching: Keep 服务员 as one service-word chunk. The teacher spends time on the ending because the nasal quality matters here.

pronunciation_focus.topic: 啤酒 / 葡萄酒 / 白酒
pronunciation_focus.coaching: The alcohol block only works if each drink remains distinct. Practice the three as one contrast set, not as isolated dictionary items.
Run the taste adjectives as a fast rhythm set so they become service-language chunks rather than five separate flashcards.
Lesson 12 explicitly treats soup and sugar as a tone contrast. Drill them together so the learner stops confusing menu language with sugar requests.
The lesson warns that often and taste sound alike here. Keep 常常 for frequency and 尝 for trying or tasting.

drills.prompt: Roleplay calling the waiter and ordering a cup of coffee with sugar.
drills.target_answer: 服务员！来一杯咖啡，加糖。
coaching_notes: Use 服务员 as the call and keep 来一杯咖啡，加糖 as one clean ordering chunk.

drills.prompt: Say in Chinese: Please bring the menu, then the bill.
coaching_notes: Lesson 12 treats these as two short service words rather than long polite sentences.

drills.prompt: Repeat the taste line: not too spicy, please.
coaching_notes: This is a high-value restaurant survival chunk.

drills.prompt: Ask in Chinese: Can you use chopsticks?
coaching_notes: Keep 用筷子 together as one tool phrase.

drills.prompt: Invite someone home and welcome them inside.
coaching_notes: This combines the invitation block with the polite-enter and polite-sit formulas.

tests.question: Which lesson-12 word means menu?
Which phrase means to drink a toast to something or someone?
Translate into Chinese: I rarely go to restaurants.
Say the question: Do you prefer bread or rice?
Which contrast pair in lesson 12 sounds similar but means soup versus sugar?

teaching_notes: Keep lesson 12 anchored in restaurant behavior, not only nouns. The real value is waiter call, order, menu, bill, and service requests.
The taste block should sound like a survival strip: 辣, 咸, 甜, 苦, 酸, and 不要太辣.
Do not let the drink section drift into random alcohol vocabulary. The useful contrast is beer, wine, baijiu, and the toast formula 为……干杯.
Link 米饭, 面包, and 面条 as a food-system contrast so the learner sees the rice-versus-flour pattern instead of memorizing isolated words.
Use 请 and 欢迎 to pivot from restaurant language into invitation language. That is what makes lesson 12 feel social rather than transactional.

lessons.title: Lesson 13: Large Numbers, Money, Dates, and Experience

lesson_lines.translation_en: Lesson 13 opens the fourth week by saying the class now needs a couple of new themes plus a full review pass.
The first big topic is extended counting: the teacher says the class can already count surprisingly far.
how many; a small number of
The lesson restates the Chinese big-to-small order: year first, then month, then date.
The teacher repeats the core spoken-sentence template: subject, time, place, action, object.

lesson_lines.zh_pinyin: wo jintian zai fanguan chi le fan.
lesson_lines.translation_en: I ate in a restaurant today.
Have you ever been to China?
The teacher compresses the particle contrast into result versus life experience.
The lesson says that spoken Chinese resolves many ambiguities through context even before writing does.

lesson_lines.zh_hanzi: 中国，汉语，俄罗斯，莫斯科，北京
lesson_lines.translation_en: China, Chinese language, Russia, Moscow, Beijing
lesson_lines.translation_ru: Китай, китайский язык, Россия, Москва, Пекин

lesson_lines.translation_en: The master explains that characters may be countless in total, but they are built from a limited stock of recurring elements.
The class now looks at how the Chinese numerals from one to ten are written.
The teacher returns from the character demo to actual usable spoken structures.
The teacher restates the order for location sentences: first the subject, then the place phrase, then the action.

lesson_lines.zh_hanzi: 我会说汉语，但是不会看汉字。
lesson_lines.zh_pinyin: wo hui shuo Hanyu, danshi bu hui kan hanzi.
lesson_lines.translation_en: I can speak Chinese, but I cannot read Chinese characters.
lesson_lines.translation_ru: Я умею говорить по-китайски, но не умею читать иероглифы.

lesson_lines.translation_en: under heaven; the world; the traditional name for China in elevated style
the world; all under heaven; China in elevated usage

lesson_lines.zh_hanzi: 第二次 / 第三次 / 第四次
lesson_lines.zh_pinyin: di er ci / di san ci / di si ci
lesson_lines.translation_en: second time / third time / fourth time
lesson_lines.translation_ru: второй раз / третий раз / четвёртый раз

lesson_lines.translation_en: The teacher closes by saying the final week will still cover travel, weather, and a few remaining high-value themes.

vocabulary.zh_pinyin: wo jintian zai fanguan chi le fan.
vocabulary.translation_en: I ate in a restaurant today.
Have you ever been to China?

vocabulary.zh_hanzi: 一 / 二 / 三 / 四 / 五
六 / 七 / 八 / 九 / 十

vocabulary.translation_en: on the table / under the table
all under heaven; the world; China in elevated usage

vocabulary.zh_pinyin: shang ge yue / xia ge yue
vocabulary.translation_ru: в прошлом месяце / в следующем месяце

vocabulary.zh_hanzi: 上个星期 / 下个星期
vocabulary.zh_pinyin: shang ge xingqi / xia ge xingqi
vocabulary.translation_ru: на прошлой неделе / на следующей неделе

vocabulary.zh_hanzi: 今年 / 明年 / 去年
vocabulary.translation_en: this year / next year / last year
vocabulary.translation_ru: в этом году / в следующем году / в прошлом году

vocabulary.zh_hanzi: 第二次 / 第三次 / 第四次
vocabulary.zh_pinyin: di er ci / di san ci / di si ci
vocabulary.translation_en: second time / third time / fourth time
vocabulary.translation_ru: второй раз / третий раз / четвёртый раз

grammar_patterns.name: Chinese large numbers pivot on 万 and 亿
grammar_patterns.explanation_en: Lesson 13 explicitly teaches the Chinese large-number ladder through 万 for ten thousand and 亿 for one hundred million, instead of the thousand-million grouping familiar from Russian or English.

grammar_patterns.name: Big to small order governs dates
grammar_patterns.explanation_en: The teacher repeats that dates in Chinese go from larger time unit to smaller one: year, then month, then date number.

grammar_patterns.name: Sentence order stays subject, time, place, action, object
grammar_patterns.explanation_en: Lesson 13 restates the core spoken-word-order pattern and demonstrates it with a restaurant sentence.

grammar_patterns.name: 了 versus 过
grammar_patterns.explanation_en: The lesson condenses the aspect contrast into result versus experience: 了 marks a completed event, while 过 marks something that has happened in one's life at some point.

grammar_patterns.name: Country, person, cuisine, and language split in different ways
grammar_patterns.explanation_en: The class reviews how the country base changes when talking about a person from that country, that country's cuisine, or that country's language.

grammar_patterns.name: Location phrases use postpositions after the noun
grammar_patterns.explanation_en: To say on the table or under the table, Chinese puts 上 or 下 after the location noun rather than before it.

grammar_patterns.name: Relative time uses spatial metaphors
grammar_patterns.explanation_en: Last and next periods are built with spatial imagery: earlier time is up or above, while future time is down or below.

grammar_patterns.name: 第 plus number plus 次 builds ordinal times
grammar_patterns.explanation_en: Lesson 13 formalizes how to say first time, second time, and so on with 第 before the number and 次 after it.

pronunciation_focus.coaching: The large-number ladder only works if 万 and 亿 stay clearly distinct. They are high-value anchor units, not decoration.
Lesson 13 explicitly uses nine versus alcohol as a tone-meaning contrast. Keep the segment pair together in practice.
The book-versus-tree pair is a useful reminder that segmental sound alone will not save you; context and character knowledge matter.
Practice 上 and 下 both as location words and as the mental model for previous versus next periods.
Treat 第一次 as one compact ordinal chunk rather than pausing after 第.

drills.prompt: Say in Chinese: Have you ever been to China?
coaching_notes: Use 过 for life experience, not 了.

drills.prompt: Say in Chinese: I ate in a restaurant today.
coaching_notes: Keep the order subject, time, place, action, object.

drills.prompt: Repeat the location pair: on the table, under the table.
coaching_notes: Use the noun first, then the postposition.

drills.prompt: Say in Chinese: last month, next month, this year, next year.
drills.target_answer: 上个月，下个月，今年，明年。
coaching_notes: This is the core time-sequence block of lesson 13.

drills.prompt: Roleplay the literacy contrast: say that you can speak Chinese but cannot read Chinese characters.
drills.target_answer: 我会说汉语，但是不会看汉字。
coaching_notes: The key contrast is speech ability versus character-reading ability.

tests.question: Which lesson-13 number unit means ten thousand?
Which particle in lesson 13 marks life experience rather than a completed result?
Translate into Chinese: On the table.
Say the phrase: the first time.
Which elevated lesson-13 term means all under heaven and can refer to China?

teaching_notes: Do not let lesson 13 dissolve into abstract explanation. Its real value is practical compression: big numbers, dates, aspect, and time-sequence phrases that the learner can reuse immediately.
Keep 万 and 亿 operational. The learner does not need every huge number in the world, but they do need the Chinese grouping logic to stop getting lost around money and dates.
Use 了 versus 过 as a meaning contrast, not a terminology lecture. Result versus experience is enough for this stage.
The character block should support the spoken course, not derail it. Keep only the pieces that help spoken learners understand ambiguity, context, and literacy.
Finish with 上个月, 下个月, 今年, 明年, 第一次, and 第四个星期 so the learner leaves with usable time-sequence tools rather than only meta-explanation.

lessons.title: Lesson 14: Weather, Temperature, Travel, and Geography

lesson_lines.translation_en: Lesson 14 announces weather as the next high-value topic because it matters for travel and daily life.
The teacher immediately combines weather with the already-known time word for today.
to be windy; for wind to blow
The temperature pattern is restated as topic first, then the number, then the degree and above-zero or below-zero marking.
The class now builds a full example for minus fifteen degrees today.
Today's temperature is fifteen degrees below zero.
to go by plane; to fly

lesson_lines.zh_hanzi: 明年我坐飞机从莫斯科到北京。
lesson_lines.zh_pinyin: mingnian wo zuo feiji cong Mosike dao Beijing.
lesson_lines.translation_en: Next year I will fly from Moscow to Beijing.
lesson_lines.translation_ru: В следующем году я полечу из Москвы в Пекин.

lesson_lines.translation_en: The class practices route building with Moscow and Saint Petersburg as the endpoints.
The lesson now shifts from weather to geography, beginning with the cardinal directions.
The teacher explains Beijing literally as the northern capital.
Nanjing is used as the southern-capital pair against Beijing.
Have you ever been to China?
Which country do you want to go to?
lesson_lines.translation_ru: В какую страну ты хочешь поехать?

lesson_lines.translation_en: What place are you going to go to?
lesson_lines.translation_ru: В какое место ты собираешься поехать?

lesson_lines.translation_en: Which city do you like most?
lesson_lines.translation_ru: Какой город тебе нравится больше всего?

lesson_lines.zh_hanzi: 我喜欢去旅行各种各样的国家。
lesson_lines.zh_pinyin: wo xihuan qu luxing gezhong geyang de guojia.
lesson_lines.translation_en: I like to travel to all kinds of countries.
lesson_lines.translation_ru: Я люблю путешествовать по разным странам.

lesson_lines.zh_pinyin: ni xihuan shenme yang de tianqi?
lesson_lines.translation_en: What kind of weather do you like?
The teacher offers rain and rainy-weather answers as a safe substitution pattern.

lesson_lines.zh_pinyin: wo qu guo Zhongguo san ci.
lesson_lines.translation_en: I have been to China three times.
lesson_lines.translation_ru: Я был в Китае три раза.

lesson_lines.translation_en: The class jokes about the sound overlap between south, man, and difficult, but the useful takeaway is still the north-south contrast.
The class now turns weather back into place-specific talk with Moscow as the reference city.

lesson_lines.zh_pinyin: ni xihuan bei feng haishi nan feng?
lesson_lines.translation_en: Do you like the north wind or the south wind?
lesson_lines.translation_ru: Тебе нравится северный ветер или южный?

lesson_lines.translation_en: The close keeps the tone warm and invitational rather than purely grammatical.

vocabulary.translation_en: Which country do you want to go to?
vocabulary.translation_ru: В какую страну ты хочешь поехать?

vocabulary.translation_en: What place are you going to go to?
vocabulary.translation_ru: В какое место ты собираешься поехать?

vocabulary.translation_en: Which city do you like most?
vocabulary.translation_ru: Какой город тебе нравится больше всего?

grammar_patterns.name: Weather statements anchor on 今天 and 天气
grammar_patterns.explanation_en: Lesson 14 teaches weather as a reusable frame: today plus weather plus a quality word or a weather event.

grammar_patterns.name: Weather events behave like verb phrases
grammar_patterns.explanation_en: Instead of saying a rain or snow noun plus a copula, the lesson uses compact event phrases like it rains, it snows, and it is windy.

grammar_patterns.name: Temperature combines 气温 with 零上 or 零下 and 度
grammar_patterns.explanation_en: The class builds temperatures by naming the temperature topic and then marking above or below zero with a degree count.

grammar_patterns.name: Travel by vehicle uses 坐 plus the transport noun
grammar_patterns.explanation_en: The lesson reuses the Chinese idea of sitting in a vehicle to express moving by car, plane, or train.

grammar_patterns.name: Routes are built with 从 ... 到 ...
grammar_patterns.explanation_en: To describe a trip from one city to another, lesson 14 uses from and to around the route before the destination settles.
examples: 明年我坐飞机从莫斯科到北京。

grammar_patterns.name: Preference questions use 喜欢 plus 什么样的 or 哪个
grammar_patterns.explanation_en: The class asks what kind of weather you like and which city you like most with the same preference logic.

grammar_patterns.name: Direction words build place names and regional labels
grammar_patterns.explanation_en: North and south are not abstract vocabulary only; they show up in Beijing, Nanjing, northerner, southerner, and wind labels.

grammar_patterns.name: 各种各样的 packages variety before the noun
grammar_patterns.explanation_en: When the teacher wants to say all kinds of countries or places, the variety phrase comes before the thing being described.

pronunciation_focus.coaching: Keep 天气 compact as one weather chunk rather than pausing after 天.
The weather verb needs to stay rhythmic and compact; do not separate 刮 from 风 into two unrelated beats.
Above zero and below zero are meaning-critical opposites. Drill them as a pair.
North and south stay central throughout the lesson in capitals, regions, and winds, so they need to be cleanly separated.
Treat regional-person labels as full chunks. Do not over-isolate 人 after the direction phrase.

drills.prompt: Say in Chinese: Today's weather is very cold.
coaching_notes: Keep 今天 before 天气 and use 很 before 冷.

drills.prompt: Ask in Chinese: Is it windy today?
coaching_notes: Use 刮风 as the weather event and close with 吗.

drills.prompt: Say in Chinese: Today's temperature is fifteen degrees below zero.
coaching_notes: This is the core temperature pattern of lesson 14.

drills.prompt: Say in Chinese: Next year I will fly from Moscow to Beijing.
drills.target_answer: 明年我坐飞机从莫斯科到北京。
coaching_notes: Use the route pair 从 ... 到 ... and the vehicle phrase 坐飞机.

drills.prompt: Ask what weather someone likes, then answer that you like hot weather.
drills.target_answer: 你喜欢什么样的天气？我喜欢热的天气。
coaching_notes: Keep the preference structure parallel in question and answer.

tests.question: Which lesson-14 word means weather?
Which phrase means below zero?
Translate into Chinese: north wind.
What generic noun does lesson 14 use for place or location?
Say the phrase: all kinds of countries.

teaching_notes: Keep lesson 14 practical. The point is not to list climate nouns in isolation but to let the learner ask, answer, and survive weather talk during travel.
Temperature is the lesson's second backbone. Drill 零上, 零下, and 度 together until the learner can produce minus and plus temperatures without translation lag.
Do not split the route block away from the weather block. Travel plus weather is the exact real-world use case the teacher is aiming at here.
Use Beijing, Nanjing, 北方人, 南方人, 北风, and 南风 as one directional family so north and south stay conceptually connected.
End with preference and comparison talk: what weather the learner likes, where they want to go, and what kind of place seems beautiful or similar.

lessons.title: Lesson 15: Seasons, Etiquette, Formal Address, and Family

lesson_lines.translation_en: Lesson 15 opens as a practical consolidation lesson with a few additional useful blocks rather than one new narrow topic.
The teacher says that seasons were left slightly aside earlier and are now worth bringing into the active core.

lesson_lines.zh_hanzi: 北 / 南 / 东 / 西

lesson_lines.translation_en: Intermediate directions are presented as combinations of the basic direction words.
The teacher immediately links the seasons block back to weather because the two belong together in actual travel talk.
Today's weather is very warm or hot.
lesson_lines.translation_ru: Сегодня очень тёплая или жаркая погода.

lesson_lines.translation_en: The lesson now says the first things that should settle almost subconsciously are the universal etiquette phrases.
it does not matter; no problem
I am very glad to meet you.
lesson_lines.translation_ru: Я очень рад познакомиться с тобой.

lesson_lines.translation_en: The teacher returns to the core rule that language sticks best when it starts from self-description and immediately useful personal questions.
The polite form 您 is introduced as a deliberate formal choice for age, rank, or respectful distance.
What is your name? in formal address
The flower metaphor is used to explain a language core plus thematic petals rather than a flat word list.
hotel; in some contexts a formal restaurant or hotel establishment
We are glad to meet you.
I work in a Chinese restaurant.

lesson_lines.zh_pinyin: wo hui shuo yi dianr Eyu.
lesson_lines.translation_en: I can speak a little Russian.

lesson_lines.zh_pinyin: wo jia you si kou ren.
lesson_lines.translation_en: There are four people in my family.
I like the Russian language very much.
I really want to go to Moscow.
lesson_lines.translation_ru: Я очень хочу поехать в Москву.

lesson_lines.zh_hanzi: 你喜欢莫斯科什么地方？
lesson_lines.translation_en: What place in Moscow do you like?
lesson_lines.translation_ru: Какое место в Москве тебе нравится?

lesson_lines.translation_en: The teacher restates that language is not only structures taken to automaticity but also the images and associations that come with it.

lesson_lines.zh_hanzi: 总是 / 有时候 / 从来

lesson_lines.translation_en: ever; in never expressions from the start of time
The class compresses the trio always, sometimes, and never into one compact practice block.

lesson_lines.zh_pinyin: wo conglai mei qu guo Zhongguo.
lesson_lines.translation_en: I have never been to China.
lesson_lines.translation_ru: Я никогда не был в Китае.

lesson_lines.translation_en: The teacher repeats that present or future negation uses 不, while past negation uses 没.
there is; to have / there is not; not have
is it or not; yes-no check
to want; to need; need to
do not want; do not need; do not
Lesson 15 explicitly points out that Chinese uses different kinship words for grandparents on the father's side and the mother's side.
The family block broadens from grandparents to grandson and granddaughter.

lesson_lines.zh_pinyin: wo laolao jiao wo ai kan shu.
lesson_lines.translation_en: My grandmother taught me to love reading books.
lesson_lines.translation_ru: Моя бабушка научила меня любить читать книги.
дедушка по материнской линии / бабушка по материнской линии

lesson_lines.translation_en: There is a metro in Tashkent.

vocabulary.zh_hanzi: 北 / 南 / 东 / 西

vocabulary.translation_en: Today's weather is very warm or hot.
I am glad to meet you.
What is your name? in polite speech

vocabulary.zh_pinyin: wo hui shuo yi dianr Eyu.
vocabulary.translation_en: I can speak a little Russian.

vocabulary.zh_pinyin: wo jia you si kou ren.
vocabulary.translation_en: There are four people in my family.
I really want to go to Moscow.
vocabulary.translation_ru: Я очень хочу поехать в Москву.

vocabulary.zh_pinyin: wo conglai mei qu guo Zhongguo.
vocabulary.translation_en: I have never been to China.
vocabulary.translation_ru: Я никогда не был в Китае.

vocabulary.translation_en: not in present-future / not in past-experience contexts
vocabulary.translation_ru: не в настоящем-будущем / не в прошедшем опыте

vocabulary.translation_en: there is; to have / there is not; not have
to need or want / do not need; do not want
There is a metro in Tashkent.

grammar_patterns.name: Seasons are built with 天
grammar_patterns.explanation_en: Lesson 15 brings the four seasons back into active use and shows that each one can be heard as a compact word ending in 天.

grammar_patterns.name: Etiquette phrases are the true language core
grammar_patterns.explanation_en: The teacher explicitly says that greetings, apologies, thanks, farewells, and meeting phrases should settle almost automatically because they start every kind of interaction.

grammar_patterns.name: Polite 您 replaces informal 你 in formal address
grammar_patterns.explanation_en: Lesson 15 contrasts the neutral name question with the respectful version that uses 您 for older or more formal situations.

grammar_patterns.name: Family size uses 有 plus a classifier for people
grammar_patterns.explanation_en: The guest dialogue uses 有 to say how many people there are in a family rather than a direct copular structure.

grammar_patterns.name: Frequency adverbs form a compact trio
grammar_patterns.explanation_en: Always, sometimes, and never are drilled together as one mental block so the learner can contrast regularity, occasionality, and zero experience quickly.

grammar_patterns.name: Past negation switches from 不 to 没
grammar_patterns.explanation_en: Lesson 15 restates that present and future negation use 不, while past or completed-experience negation uses 没.

grammar_patterns.name: Availability questions use 有 and 没有
grammar_patterns.explanation_en: When asking whether something exists or is available, the lesson uses 有 and 没有 as the practical yes-no pair.

grammar_patterns.name: Learning and teaching split into 学习 and 教
grammar_patterns.explanation_en: In the family block the teacher insists on the difference between studying yourself and teaching someone else.

pronunciation_focus.topic: 春天 / 秋天 / 冬天 / 夏天
pronunciation_focus.coaching: Drill the four seasons as a compact set so the repeated 天 ending stays stable while the first syllable changes.
These etiquette phrases should come out as full chunks, not word-by-word constructions.
Keep 您 distinct from 你 because the whole point of this block is respectful social positioning.

pronunciation_focus.topic: 总是 / 有时候 / 从来
pronunciation_focus.coaching: These three adverbs should be memorized as one contrast set rather than as isolated words.
The kinship terms sound similar enough to confuse if you learn them too late. Keep the grandmother and grandfather pair together.

drills.prompt: Repeat the four seasons in order.
coaching_notes: Keep the shared 天 ending stable.

drills.prompt: Say politely in Chinese: What is your name?
coaching_notes: Use 您, not 你, for the formal version.

drills.prompt: Say in Chinese: There are four people in my family.
coaching_notes: Use 有 for family size.

drills.prompt: Say in Chinese: I have never been to China.
coaching_notes: Past experience negation uses 没, not 不.

drills.prompt: Say in Chinese: My grandmother taught me to love reading books.
coaching_notes: Use 教 for teaching someone, not 学习.

tests.question: Which lesson-15 adverb means sometimes?
Translate into Chinese: no problem.
Which respectful pronoun replaces 你 in formal address?
Say the sentence: There is a metro in Tashkent.
Which phrase means do not need or do not want?

teaching_notes: Do not sell lesson 15 as one narrow topic. It is a consolidation lesson that intentionally braids seasons, etiquette, guest interaction, frequency adverbs, and family language together.
Keep the etiquette block near-automatic. If the learner still has to think hard about hello, sorry, thank you, and welcome, the later conversation work will stay brittle.
Use the guest dialogue as confidence training, not as a museum piece. The point is that the students really ask and answer simple but real questions.
总是, 有时候, and 从来 deserve hard drilling because they unlock much richer life-description language with very little extra grammar.
Treat the grandparent block conservatively. The transcript clearly supports 姥姥, 姥爷, 孙子, 孙女, 教, 学习, 看书, 做饭, and 地铁; do not claim cleaner kinship coverage than the lesson actually delivers.

lessons.title: Lesson 16: Final Biographies, Questions, and Closing Conversation

lesson_lines.translation_en: You all look very beautiful today!
The teacher says lesson 16 will gather together everything the class remembers, including the parts that still feel shaky.
The final task is to tell your own biography rather than repeat isolated patterns.
The teacher explicitly welcomes the part the class has already trained: asking each other questions.
This session is framed as students questioning each other, not just answering the teacher.
He describes the format as one general conversation.
The lesson is no longer supposed to feel like a rigid lesson.
The goal is to turn the class into normal human interaction using the Chinese they already have.
The first student volunteers to begin the final free-speaking round.
The student says she has a family and a daughter, turning the final lesson into an actual biography rather than a drill.

lesson_lines.zh_hanzi: 我喜欢旅行。我想去中国。
lesson_lines.zh_pinyin: wo xihuan luxing. wo xiang qu Zhongguo.
lesson_lines.translation_en: I like traveling. I want to go to China.
lesson_lines.translation_ru: Я люблю путешествовать. Я хочу поехать в Китай.

lesson_lines.translation_en: She adds that she wants to go there together with her friends.
The teacher works through the idea of having questions and keeps narrowing the line toward a clean classroom prompt.
A new mini-block begins around asking about favorite color.
The teacher repeats 颜色 several times until the color word becomes an active chunk.
He reminds the class that many different colors have already appeared before, so this is a recovery exercise, not a brand-new topic.
The color block is deliberately repetitive because the class is supposed to recall the word automatically in conversation.

lesson_lines.zh_hanzi: 你们好。我叫佩拉盖娅。我很高兴认识你们。
lesson_lines.zh_pinyin: nimen hao. wo jiao Peilageya. wo hen gaoxing renshi nimen.
lesson_lines.translation_en: Hello everyone. My name is Pelageya. I am very glad to meet you all.
lesson_lines.translation_ru: Здравствуйте. Меня зовут Пелагея. Я очень рада познакомиться со всеми вами.

lesson_lines.translation_en: The teacher helps expand the greeting so the student can compliment the whole group, not just say hello.

lesson_lines.zh_pinyin: wo qu guo Zhongguo liang ci.
lesson_lines.translation_en: I have been to China twice.
lesson_lines.translation_ru: Я был(а) в Китае два раза.

lesson_lines.zh_hanzi: 我姥姥叫露霞。我姥爷叫格纳。
lesson_lines.zh_pinyin: wo laolao jiao Luxia. wo laoye jiao Gena.
lesson_lines.translation_en: My grandmother is called Lusia. My grandfather is called Gena.
lesson_lines.translation_ru: Мою бабушку зовут Люся. Моего дедушку зовут Гена.

lesson_lines.translation_en: The student pushes the sentence toward learning to cook Chinese food, keeping the final lesson anchored in personal interests.
The class confirms that the target color is red.
The teacher checks whether the long music phrase sounds natural enough to keep.
I want to go to China.
The teacher turns the biography into a follow-up question about whether the speaker likes her work.
A short side block reminds the class how often Chinese uses a quick truth check in live conversation.
Another student begins a compact self-introduction in the final-round biography format.
She also says she likes travel and places work inside the broader Russia-China frame.

lesson_lines.zh_pinyin: wo you che, danshi bu kai che.
lesson_lines.translation_en: I have a car, but I do not drive it.
lesson_lines.translation_ru: У меня есть машина, но я не вожу её.

lesson_lines.zh_pinyin: ni de zhangfu zuo shenme gongzuo?
lesson_lines.translation_en: What work does your husband do?

lesson_lines.zh_pinyin: wo de zhangfu ye shi yanyuan.
lesson_lines.translation_en: My husband is also an actor.

lesson_lines.zh_pinyin: ni de laolao zhu zai nar?

lesson_lines.translation_en: The answer says the grandmother lives in Rostov-on-Don, and the teacher accepts the city name as-is.

lesson_lines.zh_hanzi: 我叫阿列克谢。我三十三岁。我住在莫斯科。
lesson_lines.zh_pinyin: wo jiao Aliekexie. wo sanshi san sui. wo zhu zai Mosike.
lesson_lines.translation_en: My name is Alexey. I am thirty-three years old. I live in Moscow.
lesson_lines.translation_ru: Меня зовут Алексей. Мне тридцать три года. Я живу в Москве.

lesson_lines.zh_pinyin: wo you jia. wo you pengyou.
lesson_lines.translation_en: I have a family. I have friends.
lesson_lines.translation_ru: У меня есть семья. У меня есть друзья.

lesson_lines.zh_hanzi: 我爱中国菜。我爱北京烤鸭。我每天学习汉语。
lesson_lines.zh_pinyin: wo ai Zhongguo cai. wo ai Beijing kaoya. wo meitian xuexi Hanyu.
lesson_lines.translation_en: I love Chinese food. I love Beijing duck. I study Chinese every day.
lesson_lines.translation_ru: Я люблю китайскую еду. Я люблю пекинскую утку. Я каждый день изучаю китайский.

lesson_lines.zh_hanzi: 为成功干杯。为你们干杯。
lesson_lines.zh_pinyin: wei chenggong ganbei. wei nimen ganbei.
lesson_lines.translation_en: A toast to success. A toast to all of you.
The teacher asks a new practical biography question: in which country do you work?

lesson_lines.zh_hanzi: 我在俄罗斯和中国工作。
lesson_lines.zh_pinyin: wo zai Eluosi he Zhongguo gongzuo.
lesson_lines.translation_en: I work in Russia and China.
lesson_lines.translation_ru: Я работаю в России и Китае.

lesson_lines.zh_pinyin: ni xihuan kai che kuai ma?

lesson_lines.translation_en: One more short biography round begins before the teacher closes the course.

lesson_lines.zh_pinyin: wo jia you si kou ren.
lesson_lines.translation_en: There are four people in my family.
The teacher checks whether the speaker can cook Chinese food, keeping the closing conversation practical and personal.

lesson_lines.zh_pinyin: wo bu hui zuo Zhongguo cai.
lesson_lines.translation_ru: Я не умею готовить китайскую еду.

lesson_lines.translation_en: The teacher says the real magic is that a pile of words slowly stops being a pile and starts becoming thoughts, feelings, and images.
He says there comes a point when we stop seeing letters and grammar alone and start wanting to know something through the language.
The time was short and they could not master everything, but something decisive still happened.
The language has stopped feeling alien because the class has already touched it directly.
Another dimension has opened up in which the learners can already feel reasonably comfortable.
Languages are for pleasure, for communication, for travel, and for understanding the surrounding world.
The teacher says goodbye to the class and thanks them at the end of the course.

vocabulary.translation_en: I am very glad to meet you all
vocabulary.translation_ru: я очень рад познакомиться со всеми вами

vocabulary.zh_pinyin: wo qu guo Zhongguo liang ci
vocabulary.translation_en: I have been to China twice
vocabulary.translation_ru: я был(а) в Китае два раза
бабушка по материнской линии / дедушка по материнской линии

vocabulary.translation_en: I want to go to China

vocabulary.zh_pinyin: ni de zhangfu zuo shenme gongzuo?
vocabulary.translation_en: What does your husband do for work?

vocabulary.zh_pinyin: wo de zhangfu ye shi yanyuan
vocabulary.translation_en: My husband is also an actor

vocabulary.zh_hanzi: 我在俄罗斯和中国工作
vocabulary.zh_pinyin: wo zai Eluosi he Zhongguo gongzuo
vocabulary.translation_en: I work in Russia and China
vocabulary.translation_ru: я работаю в России и Китае

vocabulary.zh_pinyin: ni xihuan kai che kuai ma?
wo jia you si kou ren
vocabulary.translation_en: There are four people in my family

vocabulary.zh_pinyin: wo bu hui zuo Zhongguo cai
vocabulary.translation_ru: я не умею готовить китайскую еду

vocabulary.zh_hanzi: 为成功干杯 / 为你们干杯
vocabulary.zh_pinyin: wei chenggong ganbei / wei nimen ganbei
vocabulary.translation_en: a toast to success / a toast to all of you

grammar_patterns.name: Final biography mode chains simple clauses
grammar_patterns.explanation_en: Lesson 16 stops introducing isolated mini-topics and instead asks learners to chain name, place, family, likes, plans, and work into one flowing self-introduction.

grammar_patterns.name: Question exchange uses 有问题吗 and live follow-ups
grammar_patterns.explanation_en: The teacher explicitly turns the room toward peer questions, not only teacher prompts, and uses 有问题吗 as a launch point for freer dialogue.

grammar_patterns.name: 为什么 ... 因为 ... remains the compact reasoning pair
grammar_patterns.explanation_en: The lesson revisits why-because not as abstract grammar but as the quickest way to justify taste, travel, or work choices inside real biographies.

grammar_patterns.name: Experience uses 去过 plus frequency counting
grammar_patterns.explanation_en: The class says not only that someone has been to China but that it happened twice, showing experiential grammar plus count expression in one sentence.

grammar_patterns.name: Preference speech is built on 喜欢 plus an object or activity
grammar_patterns.explanation_en: Final conversation practice repeatedly leans on 喜欢 to describe food, reading, music, travel, colors, cars, and other personal preferences.

grammar_patterns.name: Possession and family counting use 有
grammar_patterns.explanation_en: The final biographies keep family language compact with 有 for having family members and for counting how many people belong to the household.

grammar_patterns.name: Work questions combine 做什么工作 and location with 在
grammar_patterns.explanation_en: The lesson asks where a person works and what work a spouse does, combining profession questions with location framing.
examples: 我在俄罗斯和中国工作。

grammar_patterns.name: Toasts use 为 ... 干杯 in a live social setting
grammar_patterns.explanation_en: The course closes with a real toast pattern instead of a dry grammar illustration, which is exactly the kind of social chunk Petrov-style practice wants to automate.

pronunciation_focus.topic: 你们好 / 你们今天都很美
pronunciation_focus.coaching: Keep 你们 as one fast greeting chunk and do not flatten 都很美 into word-by-word stress.
These two short reasoning words need to be heard and answered as a pair without hesitation.
Both words show how quickly a conversation dies if the learner cannot retrieve a high-frequency topic noun on demand.

pronunciation_focus.topic: 莫斯科 / 俄罗斯 / 中国 / 北京
pronunciation_focus.coaching: Proper nouns should be drilled as confident chunks because the final lesson makes learners speak about real places repeatedly.

pronunciation_focus.topic: 谢谢老师 / 为成功干杯
pronunciation_focus.coaching: The closing social formulas should sound natural and energetic, not carefully assembled syllable by syllable.

drills.prompt: Repeat the opening greeting and compliment as one smooth teacher chunk.
drills.target_answer: 你们好！你们今天都很美！
coaching_notes: Keep it warm and social, not theatrical.

drills.prompt: Say in Chinese: Do you have any questions?
coaching_notes: Use 有 plus 问题 before 吗.

drills.prompt: Say in Chinese: I have been to China twice.
coaching_notes: Keep 去过 and 两次 together.

drills.prompt: Introduce yourself in Chinese with three clauses: where you live, what you like, and where you want to go.
drills.target_answer: 我住在莫斯科。我喜欢旅行。我想去中国。
coaching_notes: This is the core final-lesson biography pattern.

drills.prompt: Give the final toast and thank-you line.
coaching_notes: Keep the toast energetic and the farewell clean.

tests.question: Which lesson-16 pair means why and because?
Translate into Chinese: What is your dog's name?
Say in Chinese: I work in Russia and China.
tests.answer_key: 我在俄罗斯和中国工作。

tests.question: Which phrase means all kinds of music?
Give the final closing response to the teacher.

teaching_notes: Do not teach lesson 16 as one more topic lesson. It is the course wrap-up where the learner is supposed to speak as a person, not just fill grammar slots.
The safest way to use this lesson is biography plus follow-up questions: name, city, family, travel, likes, work, and one toast or farewell line.
Keep why-because, favorite color, and music preference short. They are there to keep a conversation alive, not to turn into abstract explanation blocks.
The closing reflection matters. It explains that the real gain is not perfect recall but the feeling that the language has stopped being alien.
Do not overclean the final chorus. 谢谢老师 is simple, repetitive, and emotionally appropriate exactly as it appears.
